Analysis.description:
Structured simulation analysis.

Lighter than :class:`ltagent.ir.Analysis`: the graph only needs
enough to let the converter emit a SPICE directive. Field names
match the IR's so the converter is a 1:1 mapping.
Analysis.title: Analysis

AnalysisKind.description: Simulation analysis kind supported by the graph layer.
AnalysisKind.title: AnalysisKind

Component.description:
One circuit element.

The graph component is decoupled from :class:`ltagent.ir.Component`:
it has no ``spicePrefix`` (the converter derives it from ``kind``)
and no ``nodes`` list (the pin map is the canonical connectivity
view). This keeps the model friendly to incremental editing
where the AI may add pins or change pin ordering without
confusing the layout generator.

id.description: Unique component id, e.g. R1, C1, Vin
id.title: Id

value.description: SPICE value string. Required for sources; for resistor / capacitor / inductor it is the value (e.g. '1k'). For diode / npn / pnp / nmos / pmos it is the SPICE model name (e.g. '1N4148', 'BC547'). For opamp it is the subcircuit name (e.g. 'UniversalOpamp').
value.title: Value

model.description: Optional SPICE model name. When set on semiconductor kinds it takes precedence over ``value``.
model.title: Model

role.description: Semantic role used by the layout and topology layers, e.g. ``series_resistor``, ``shunt_capacitor``.
role.title: Role

pins.description: Mapping from pin name to net name.

Component.title: Component

ComponentKind.description:
Semantic kind of a circuit element.

Mirrors :class:`ltagent.ir.ComponentKind` so the graph -> IR
converter is a 1:1 mapping. New kinds must be added in both
places at the same time.
ComponentKind.title: ComponentKind

Constraints.description:
Optional design targets (targetCutoffHz, targetGain, ...).

Values must be flat scalars (str / int / float / bool) so the
JSON shape is reviewable and the Math Core can read it without
its own schema. This mirrors :class:`ltagent.ir.Constraints`.
Constraints.title: Constraints

Directive.description:
A curated raw SPICE directive.

The graph model stores the directive as a structured object
(rather than a bare list of strings) so the converter can
distinguish between the directive token and its arguments
without re-parsing. The token is allowlist-validated.

name.description: The directive token including the leading dot, e.g. '.tran', '.op'. Must be in DIRECTIVE_ALLOWLIST.
name.title: Name

args.description: Free-form argument string after the directive token. Empty by default; the converter passes the string through unchanged.
args.title: Args

Directive.title: Directive

LayoutHint.description:
Optional hints for schematic generation.

Hints are non-binding: the deterministic layout in
:mod:`ltagent.asc` may override them. The graph just records
what the AI / planner thinks the flow should look like.

flow.description: Preferred schematic flow, e.g. ``left_to_right``, ``top_to_bottom``. Recognised values are ``left_to_right`` and ``top_to_bottom``; any other value is treated as ``left_to_right`` by the layout.
flow.title: Flow

inputNode.description: Net name expected to be at the schematic's input side.
inputNode.title: Inputnode

outputNode.description: Net name expected to be at the schematic's output side.
outputNode.title: Outputnode

anchors.description: Optional per-component placement hints. Keys are component ids; values are free-form anchor names used by the deterministic layout. Unknown values are ignored.
anchors.title: Anchors

LayoutHint.title: LayoutHint

Measurement.description: A ``.meas`` expression to extract after simulation.
Measurement.title: Measurement

Net.description:
A named electrical net.

The graph only stores the name and role; connectivity is encoded
in each :class:`Component`'s :class:`PinMap`. The graph model
also tolerates a net being declared but not (yet) connected --
that is reported as a warning by :func:`validate_graph`, not as
a hard error.

name.description: Net name, must match the safe SPICE pattern
name.title: Name

aliases.description: Optional human-friendly aliases used by the schematic renderer. Each alias must match the safe SPICE pattern.
aliases.title: Aliases

Net.title: Net

NetType.description:
Electrical role of a net.

* ``signal``  -- regular signal net (input, output, internal).
* ``ground``  -- the reference net. At most one such net per
  graph; its name must equal :data:`GROUND_NODE` (i.e. ``"0"``).
* ``power``   -- supply rail (e.g. ``vcc``, ``vee``). Reserved
  for Phase 11/12 work; accepted here so layout hints can refer
  to supply rails before the graph -> IR converter is taught to
  emit them.
NetType.title: NetType

PinMap.description:
Mapping from a component's pin names to net names.

The graph does not store pin position, length, or any other
layout data: layout lives in the ``asc`` writer. Pin names are
SPICE-identifier style (``+``/``-`` for sources, ``1``/``2`` for
two-terminal passives, ``in+``/``in-``/``out`` for op-amp
subcircuits). Net names follow :data:`NODE_NAME_PATTERN`.

pins.description: Map of pin name to net name. Pin names follow the permissive ^[A-Za-z0-9_+\-][A-Za-z0-9_+\-]*$ so SPICE pin shapes such as ``1``/``2`` (passives), ``+``/``-`` (sources), and ``in+``/``out`` (op-amp subcircuits) are all accepted. Net names must match ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_.]*$|^0$. The map may be empty for subcircuit placeholders but most concrete kinds need at least one entry.
pins.title: Pins

PinMap.title: PinMap

description: Workbench v2 contract for AnalogGraph. Generated from the Pydantic model in src/ltagent/workbench_v2.py. The Python validators remain the source of truth; this JSON Schema is a first-pass machine-readable contract.

domain.description: Domain hint. Only ``analog`` is recognised in Phase 1. Other domains (e.g. ``sensor``) are reserved for later phases and will be added in lockstep with the topology library.
domain.title: Domain

topology.description: Optional topology name (e.g. ``rc_lowpass``). Empty string is allowed while the topology is being assembled.
topology.title: Topology

components.description: Map of component id to component. Keys are the same as each component's ``id`` field; both are kept for fast lookup and to keep the JSON shape stable for the AI.
components.title: Components

nets.description: Map of net name to net. The ground net, when present, must be named ``0`` and have ``type == ground``.
nets.title: Nets

title: AnalogGraph
